title: Vendored Dependency Management

sidebar_label: Dependency Updates
description: Discover, review, and update vendored dependencies locally with Atmos
---
import Intro from '@site/src/components/Intro'

<Intro>
Manage vendored dependencies from your workstation: discover eligible updates, review their
upstream changes, update the pinned version, and pull the resulting sources with Atmos.
</Intro>

Atmos brings the dependency-management loop into the CLI. You can make and review dependency
updates locally before deciding whether to include them in a pull request.

:::note Legacy Component Updater GitHub Action

`cloudposse/github-action-atmos-component-updater` is deprecated. Its documentation remains
available as a [legacy reference](/deprecated/github-actions/component-updater).

:::

## Discover eligible updates

Start with a dry run. Atmos checks each Git-backed dependency against its version constraints and
reports the updates it would make without changing files:

```shell
atmos vendor update --check
```

## Review dependency changes

Use `atmos vendor diff` to inspect the source changes before accepting an update:

```shell
# Compare the pinned version with the latest tag.
atmos vendor diff --component vpc

# Compare two explicit versions, optionally limited to one file.
atmos vendor diff --component vpc --from 1.0.0 --to 2.0.0 --diff-file variables.tf
```

## Update dependency pins

Update all eligible Git-backed dependencies, one component, or a tag-selected set:

```shell
# Update every eligible dependency in the manifest.
atmos vendor update

# Update one component or all components carrying a tag.
atmos vendor update --component vpc
atmos vendor update --tags networking

# Update dependency pins, then pull the updated component sources.
atmos vendor update --pull
```

Atmos updates the pinned version in place while preserving comments, YAML anchors, templates,
and formatting. It follows the same precedence as `atmos vendor pull`: a repository-level
`vendor.yaml` takes precedence, with support for per-component `component.yaml` manifests when
no matching vendor source exists.

## Version policy

Each source can constrain the versions Atmos may select, including semantic-version ranges,
excluded releases, and prerelease handling. See the
[`atmos vendor update` reference](/cli/commands/vendor/vendor-update#version-constraints) for
the full configuration and flag reference.
